concept_ref: §3 row 5 — 'students routinely satisfy (1) while violating (2) or (3)'

scenario.title: Emergency Override
scenario.narrative: An emergency system triggers when alarm A fires, or when both backup sensor B and manual override C are active.

claim: Toggling each condition once in isolation is enough for MCDC.
hints: Re-read Law Card 6. The trap: an 'isolation' test where you flip a condition is NOT a valid pair if the outcome doesn't actually flip.
Look at B: T2↔T3 only flips B, but the outcome stays false because A=false AND C=false make B's contribution zero. This is NOT a valid independence pair for B.
Solution — A: T1↔T2 valid. B: needs C=true so flipping B matters; e.g. add T5={A=F,B=T,C=T,out=T} and pair T4↔T5. C: similarly needs B=true. The given 4-test set FAILS MCDC for B and C.

seeded_faults.description: Manual override C is ignored when backup sensor B is inactive

misconceptions.explanation_md: For `A || (B && C)`, testing each condition alone in isolation may not produce a valid independence pair because the decision may not flip. You need pairs where exactly one condition changes AND the decision flips.
